description: Single source of truth for Sonar rule suppressions across SonarCloud, Codacy, and local builds

rules.reason: Methods should not have too many parameters - high-performance SIMD methods require multiple parameters for zero-allocation patterns
Unused private types or members should be removed - false positives on reflection/serialization patterns
Floating point numbers should not be tested for equality - intentional exact comparisons in financial calculations
Inappropriate casts should not be made - false positives on generic constraint patterns
Hashes should include unpredictable salt - not applicable to non-cryptographic hashing
Using pseudorandom number generators is security-sensitive - GBM uses deterministic seeds for reproducibility
Null pointers should not be dereferenced - false positives with nullable reference types
Conditionally executed code should be reachable - false positives on defensive programming patterns
Boolean expressions should not be gratuitous - false positives on explicit clarity patterns
Caller information arguments should not be provided explicitly - intentional for testing/debugging
Cipher Block Chaining IVs should be unpredictable - not applicable to non-cryptographic code
Member initializer values should not be redundant - null! is intentional for nullable reference types in Quantower adapters
Empty nullable value should not be accessed - false positives with HasValue checks
Cognitive Complexity of methods should not be too high - complex algorithms require complex implementations
Calculations should not overflow - false positives on checked arithmetic contexts
Objects should not be disposed more than once - false positives on defensive dispose patterns
Empty collections should not be accessed or iterated - false positives on lazy initialization
Secure random number generators should not output predictable values - deterministic seeds for reproducibility
Types allowed to be deserialized should be restricted - not applicable to this codebase
JWT tokens should not be created using insecure secrets - not applicable to this codebase
